units.verbatim_text: God has been dealing with us about things that have kept us bound. You know you can be a Bible-believing, tongue-talking, Spirit-filled child of God but still be held in bondage in some area of your life, right? Are you guys with me this morning? You know my way, you need to talk back to me, we need to interact. And so today we are going into the series finale of the road to freedom. If you've been blessed by this series, can you make some noise quickly?
units.context: [Banky introduces the sermon series finale on freedom, emphasizing bondage despite faith.]

units.verbatim_text: The thief comes not but to steal, to kill, and to destroy. The only people that can never get free are people that don't realize they're in bondage in the first place. And that's what we've been dealing with all along. So today we decided that the series finale is going to be on the prison of pornography. Amen. I got one amen, God bless you, because it's a touchy subject. It's a sensitive issue. It's something that a lot of people are not comfortable talking about.
units.context: [Banky introduces the topic of pornography as a form of bondage that many are unaware of.]

units.verbatim_text: When it comes to the topic of pornography, people who deal with it fall into one of two categories. The first group of people think that it's not a big deal, it's okay, it's normal. We believe that, you know, guys, it's just what guys do. And girls, yes, and girls. So we say things like, oh, I just watch a little bit. We say things like, I use it for stress relief or when I'm horny. But it's a lie because the person that will get hurt first of all is yourself.
units.context: [Banky discusses common justifications for pornography use and its self-destructive nature.]

units.verbatim_text: The devil thrives in that era of shame, in that darkness. So he wants to keep you in that place where you think that you are the only one that is dealing with it and you're the only one that is struggling with it. And so you stay there. The devil uses that shame to keep us in chains, but those chains will break today in the mighty name of Jesus. When it comes to the addiction to pornography and the lust of the flesh, the devil is after our relationships, our finances, our mental health, our destiny.
units.context: [Banky explains how shame keeps people trapped in pornography addiction.]

units.verbatim_text: Studies have shown that many married men that struggle with erectile dysfunction, I'm sorry if this is too graphic, but we are grown in the Water Brook, can we tell the truth? Many married men who struggle with erectile dysfunction, where they are unable to have fulfilling sexual healthy relationships with their wives, you can trace it back to years of porn usage where they've rewired their brains. So you are gaining more pleasure from the idea of a woman or many women than the woman that you have in real life.
units.context: [Banky discusses the impact of pornography on marital relationships and sexual health.]

units.verbatim_text: Pornography gets its name from the Greek word 'pornographos' which means the writing or the display of prostitution. It has evolved so much over the years and it has become so commonplace that, like I said before, it's not really viewed as a big deal. Half of our music videos now, they're kind of soft porn. Let's call it a spade a spade. And I'm speaking not from a self-righteous place because me too, I made some interesting videos.
units.context: [Banky critiques the normalization of pornography in media and its cultural impact.]

units.verbatim_text: The enemy is targeting our children. He's targeting the minds of children from a very young age and so we have to be vigilant because porn is a gateway and it is a very slippery slope. I'm speaking from personal experience. It is a gateway, it is a slippery slope. Start off thinking it's not a big deal, but for many people, you end up discovering the hard way that this thing is as powerful, if not more powerful, than drug addiction and alcohol addiction.
units.context: [Banky warns about the dangers of pornography as a gateway addiction affecting youth.]

units.verbatim_text: Lust can never satisfy. Did you hear what I said? Lust can never satisfy. Only love can satisfy. So what starts with the occasional watching of a video from time to time, then you realize you need to watch a few videos or you're clicking through in quick succession. You are now carving out time in your schedule. You determine your productivity. You are watching more, watching more, and then even the kinds of content that you watch is changing.
units.context: [Banky explains the insatiable nature of lust and its escalating impact on behavior.]
